name: Diethylcarbamazine (DEC)
description: Synthetic organic compound used as a highly effective microfilaricidal agent. Disrupts microfilarial membrane arachidonic acid metabolism, rendering them highly susceptible to rapid phagocytosis and clearance by the host immune system.
preparation: Oral tablets (50mg or 100mg). The standard dosage is 6 mg/kg daily in divided doses after meals for 12 days, or as a single annual dose (in combination with albendazole) during mass drug administration (MDA) programs.
safetyAlert: Can trigger severe systemic inflammatory reactions (Mazzotti reaction) in patients co-infected with Onchocerciasis (river blindness) due to rapid microfilaria clearance. Always screen for ocular microfilariae before initiating.
interactions: Immunosuppressants may reduce therapeutic clearance rates. Acts highly synergistically when co-administered with Albendazole.
